title: Fabiano Scherner

*5th degree black belt in [Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu](/wiki/Brazilian_Jiu-Jitsu "Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu")*[[1]](#cite_note-1)

(born June 30, 1972)[[12]](#cite_note-12) is a Brazilian former professional [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_artist "Mixed martial artist") and [Brazilian jiu-jitsu](/wiki/Brazilian_jiu-jitsu "Brazilian jiu-jitsu") [black belt](/wiki/Brazilian_jiu-jitsu_ranking_system#Black_belt "Brazilian jiu-jitsu ranking system"). He has fought in [Cage Rage](/wiki/Cage_Rage "Cage Rage"), [IFL](/wiki/International_Fight_League "International Fight League") and the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"). Scherner is a Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu competitor who won multiple championships in all belt ranks on international and world stage in adult and master divisions.[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)
+ ISKA MMA Americas Heavyweight Championship (One time)

**Fabiano Scherner** (born June 30, 1972)[[12]](#cite_note-12) is a Brazilian former professional [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_artist "Mixed martial artist") and [Brazilian jiu-jitsu](/wiki/Brazilian_jiu-jitsu "Brazilian jiu-jitsu") [black belt](/wiki/Brazilian_jiu-jitsu_ranking_system#Black_belt "Brazilian jiu-jitsu ranking system"). He has fought in [Cage Rage](/wiki/Cage_Rage "Cage Rage"), [IFL](/wiki/International_Fight_League "International Fight League") and the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"). Scherner is a Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu competitor who won multiple championships in all belt ranks on international and world stage in adult and master divisions.[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)

## Background

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fabiano_Scherner&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Background")]

Scherner was born in [Tres Passos](/wiki/Tres_Passos "Tres Passos") on June 30, 1972. In 1999, Scherner started training Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu in Brazil with [Carlson Gracie](/wiki/Carlson_Gracie "Carlson Gracie") student Murilo Rupp. There he was introduced to [Mario Sperry](/wiki/Mario_Sperry "Mario Sperry"), Murilo Bustamante, and Luis Roberto "Bebeo" Duarte. Bebeo was one of the founding members of the Brazilian Top Team (BTT) academy and invited Scherner to train with BTT.[[15]](#cite_note-MMA-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16)

Scherner received his black belt in a very fast three years and nine months. Shortly following this, Ricardo Liborio invited Scherner to start training MMA and teaching BJJ in [Olympia, Washington](/wiki/Olympia,_Washington "Olympia, Washington"). While in Olympia, Scherner began cross-training with fellow heavyweight [Jeff Monson](/wiki/Jeff_Monson "Jeff Monson"), as he worked to develop his MMA skills.[[15]](#cite_note-MMA-15)

## Mixed martial arts career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fabiano_Scherner&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts career")]

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fabiano_Scherner&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

Scherner made his UFC debut against [Brandon Vera](/wiki/Brandon_Vera "Brandon Vera"), losing via TKO (knees & punches) in the second round.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

In his second fight in the UFC, Scherner fought [Gabriel Gonzaga](/wiki/Gabriel_Gonzaga "Gabriel Gonzaga"), losing via TKO (Punches) in the second round. Following the loss, Scherner was released from the UFC.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

### IFL

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fabiano_Scherner&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: IFL")]

After leaving the UFC, Scherner fought against [Gilbert Yvel](/wiki/Gilbert_Yvel "Gilbert Yvel") at [Cage Rage 17 - Ultimate Challenge](/wiki/Cage_Rage_Championships "Cage Rage Championships") losing via TKO (Punches) in the first round. Scherner then fought in three other organizations in Brazil Before fighting in the IFL, racking up 2 wins and 1 no contest.

In 2008, Scherner fought [Roy Nelson](/wiki/Roy_Nelson_(fighter) "Roy Nelson (fighter)") for the [IFL Heavyweight Championship](/wiki/List_of_IFL_champions "List of IFL champions") at IFL – [Las Vegas](/wiki/Las_Vegas_Valley "Las Vegas Valley") losing via TKO (Punches) in the first round.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

### Post-IFL

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fabiano_Scherner&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Post-IFL")]

Scherner returned to action four months later against Journeyman Mychal Clark who submitted Scherner with punches from a mounted position.

Scherner then fought Tracy Willis, and was knocked down by an overhand right early in the second round. Willis followed up with hammerfists that opened a cut on Scherner's forehead and prompted referee Josh Rosenthal to stop the fight.

Scherner fought Nick Braker on June 6, 2009, at Carnage at the Creek 6 winning via Submission (Rear-Naked Choke) at 3:08 of the first round.

Scherner lost his next fight against heavyweight prospect [Mike Hayes](/wiki/Mike_Hayes_(fighter) "Mike Hayes (fighter)") via KO (Punch) at 3:50 of round 2.

Scherner fought Scott Junk at Galaxy MMA 1 – Worlds Collide, During the fight Scherner landed an unintentional low blow on Junk that dropped the [Heavyweight](/wiki/Heavyweight_(MMA) "Heavyweight (MMA)") to his knees. Junk laid on the cage floor for several minutes before he informed the ringside doctors he could no long continue. The fight was ruled a no contest with the official end coming just 27 seconds into round one.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

Scherner was scheduled to fight fellow UFC veteran [Brad Morris](/wiki/Brad_Morris "Brad Morris") in [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia") at Brace For War 6 on November 13,[[21]](#cite_note-21) but he pulled out of the fight due to travel issues. He was replaced by Josh Ogilvie.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

On January 20, 2012, Scherner entered WCFC: Last Man Standing 2, a 16 fighter, last-man-standing style MMA tournament. After winning his first fight, Scherner lost to the tournament winner Tyler East, in the final and main event.[[23]](#cite_note-23)

On August 4, 2012, Scherner faced Anthony "Freight Train" Hamilton at SportFight 31. After connecting with a right and dropping him to the canvas, Scherner continued to throw strikes until Hamilton gave up his neck and Scherner was able to finish with an arm triangle choke, providing Hamilton with his only defeat via submission.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

Less than 2 months later, on September 17, 2012, Scherner faced against the much younger Vitaly Minakov, losing via TKO (punches) late in the first round of the main event.[[25]](#cite_note-25)

* **Submission Underground - SUG**
